section_name: Suspension or Revocation of Branch Pilot’s License
chapter_title: Jefferson & Orange Cnty. Pilots Licensing and Regul. Act

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: On complaint or on its own motion, and after notice and hearing, the board may suspend a branch pilot’s license for not more than six months or recommend that the governor revoke a branch pilot’s license if the board finds that the pilot has:
failed to demonstrate and maintain the qualifications for a license required by this chapter;
used narcotics or other types of drugs, chemicals, or controlled substances as defined by law that impair the pilot’s ability to perform the pilot’s duties skillfully and efficiently;
used alcohol to an extent that impairs the pilot’s ability to perform the pilot’s duties skillfully and efficiently;
violated a provision of this chapter or rules adopted by the board under this chapter;
made a material misstatement in the application for a license;
obtained or attempted to obtain a license under this chapter by fraud or misrepresentation;
charged a pilotage rate other than that approved by the board;

intentionally refused to pilot a vessel when requested to do so by the master or person responsible for navigation of the vessel except when, in the judgment of the pilot, movement of the vessel would have constituted a hazard to life or property or when pilotage charges that are due and owing are unpaid by the person ordering the pilot services;
been absent from duty in violation of board rules and without authorization;
aided or abetted another pilot in failing to perform the other pilot’s duties;
been guilty of carelessness, neglect of duty, intentional unavailability for normal performance of duties, refusal to perform duties, misconduct, or incompetence while on duty;
intentionally failed to comply with an order of the board; or
intentionally caused damage to a vessel.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: On determining that a license should be suspended or revoked, the board shall adopt a written order that states its findings and:
suspends the license for a stated period; or
recommends to the governor revocation of the license.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: If the federal pilot’s license of a pilot licensed under this chapter is suspended or revoked, the board, on a finding that it has good cause, shall suspend the license for the same period or revoke the license under this chapter.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The governor, on receipt of a board order recommending revocation of a license, shall revoke the license.

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: A suspension of a license on the recommendation of a pilot review board takes effect on adoption of the board’s order. A revocation of a branch pilot’s license takes effect on issuance of the governor’s decision.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 745, Sec. 1, eff. June 20, 2003.
